soap:
RE-REVIEW · lance-format/lance#6255 — feat: add transaction-level table_metadata_updates
reviewed 6a59ee74 → 22dddee2  (64 commits in range; 12 PR-owned files; 191 base-merge files excluded)

CONDITIONS LEDGER (0/1 met)

✗ not_met  [blocking] — All protobuf updates must be voted on by the PMC before merge, since they are a permanent format change (https://lance.org/community/voting/)
  → The format-changing field is still present at head. `protos/transaction.proto` net delta `6a59ee7...22dddee` adds `optional UpdateMap table_metadata_updates = 9;` inside `message Transaction` (delta hunk `@@ -248,6 +248,12 @@`; confirmed at transaction.proto:256). No PMC vote is recorded in the PR thread, issue comments, or timeline.
  → `author_resolved` is false, and the thread does not support a resolution. The author (fecet) replied: "I'm fully sold on the Action direction [#5960] — strictly cleaner than the field this PR introduces" and proposed a non-format-changing interim (reserve a `__table_metadata__.*` prefix in `transaction_properties`). The author has effectively conceded the proto change should be withdrawn, yet the field remains in the diff. Condition cannot be met while the unvoted proto change ships.

FRESH-EYES DELTA

The net delta from last review is dominated by (a) carrier plumbing for the new proto field — `python/src/transaction.rs`, `python/python/lance/dataset.py` `Update.table_metadata_updates`, `java/lance-jni/src/transaction.rs` — and (b) a large body of unrelated scanner/blob work pulled into the same range (`to_pandas(blob_mode=...)`, `batch_size_bytes`, `read_blobs`, `prewarm_index(with_position=...)`). The unrelated work is scope creep but not new-and-bad in itself.

New concerns in the metadata-updates path (drafted as issue comments):

- issue(blocking): The conflict-detection logic that guards this feature (`rust/lance/src/io/commit/conflict_resolver.rs`, `rust/lance/src/dataset/transaction.rs`) is net-unchanged across `6a59ee7...head` (+0 −0). The new `table_metadata_updates` conflict check only compares transaction-level field vs transaction-level field; a concurrent `UpdateConfig` carrying a metadata write lives inside `operation`, and `Append`↔`UpdateConfig` is allowed — so two commits mutating the same metadata key can both succeed, later rebase silently wins. Pre-existing gap, but the feature relies on it.

- issue(blocking): `replace=true` semantics defeat `keys_overlap()`: a `replace` map and an incremental update to a disjoint key are treated as compatible, but the rebasing transaction discards the other's entire metadata. Same caveat — logic is net-unchanged in-delta.

- issue(non-blocking): `execute_batch()` synthesizes the merged append transaction with `table_metadata_updates: None`, so `commit_batch()` callers batching append+cursor would commit the data and silently drop every metadata mutation — breaking the stated atomic-append use case.

RECOMMENDATION: block on 1 condition (the unvoted proto format change), plus the feature's correctness rests on conflict-detection gaps that remain unaddressed in the delta.
  confidence: high on the block — the format-changing field is present at head with no PMC vote, and the author has signaled intent to abandon it for the #5960 Action design. what would make this wrong: a PMC vote recorded outside the PR (mailing list / discussion) that I cannot see from the GitHub API, or a later commit beyond 22dddee removing the field.
